description: CodeBoarding is an architecture-analysis and documentation pipeline that scans a codebase, performs deterministic static analysis (via LSP and project scanning), and uses a multi-agent LLM-driven reasoning layer to produce architectural descriptions, diagrams, and health diagnostics. The CLI/orchestration layer coordinates scanning, plugin loading, incremental state handling, and agent execution; the static analysis engine extracts symbols, references, and call graphs; the multi-agent system generates high-level and detailed documentation using the analysis data; incremental state & persistence caches results and enables impact-driven re-analysis; and the code health module consumes static data to produce maintainability and quality reports. The primary flow: CLI triggers scanning and LSP-based parsing, the static engine persists results to the state layer, agents query that data to generate architecture outputs, and health diagnostics augment the final reports returned to the CLI for rendering and persistence.

components.name: CLI & Orchestration Layer
components.description: Primary entry point and coordinator. Manages lifecycle, configuration, plugin registry, and orchestrates flows between scanning, analysis, agents, and output rendering (documentation and diagrams).

components.name: CLI Entry & Environment Manager
components.description: Primary interface for the user; handles CLI argument parsing, user configuration loading, and environment bootstrapping. Verifies external dependencies and language servers before analysis begins.

components.name: CLI Command Orchestrator
components.description: Primary entry point and workflow controller. Defines the CLI schema, enforces argument constraints (e.g., local vs remote), and routes execution to local processing or remote cloning and analysis.

components.name: Tooling & Dependency Provisioner
components.description: Manages external tool lifecycles and user configurations: bootstraps NPM, resolves/installs missing language servers (LSPs), and injects API keys from config.toml into the runtime environment.

components.name: Environment State & Path Resolver
components.description: Utilities for filesystem navigation and state persistence. Resolves project roots, manages temporary directories for remote clones, and uses MetaCache to monitor environment health and staleness of analyzed artifacts.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator
components.description: Central engine managing the high-level analysis workflow. Coordinates scanning, incremental change detection, diagram generation, and caching to determine which parts of the codebase need re-evaluation.

components.name: Project Discovery & Language Adapter
components.description: Scans the target repository to identify source files and determine the project's language stack; applies language-specific configurations and ignore patterns to build an inventory for analysis.

components.name: Plugin & Registry Manager
components.description: Maintains the registry of available tools, agents, and plugins. Enables dynamic loading of components so the orchestrator can invoke extensions without hard-coded dependencies.

components.name: Execution Monitoring & Tracing
components.description: Cross-cutting telemetry that records execution metrics, monitors LLM token consumption, and provides tracing of orchestration steps for debugging and cost management.

components.name: Documentation & CI Integration
components.description: Transforms analysis results into human-readable outputs (Markdown, Mermaid diagrams) and manages CI/CD integration (e.g., GitHub Actions) to automate documentation updates.

components.name: Static Analysis Engine
components.description: Deterministic engine that interacts with LSP to extract symbols, references, and call graphs and uses a project scanner to identify languages/files, producing the raw structural data consumed by agents and other modules.

components.name: Project Discovery & Environment Scanner
components.description: Scans the filesystem to detect programming languages, configuration files, and classifies files to determine which LSP servers and analysis steps to run.

components.name: Analysis Orchestrator & LSP Interface
components.description: Central controller managing the analysis lifecycle, JSON-RPC communication with language servers, batching of requests for definitions/references, and the orchestration of analysis tasks.

components.name: Semantic Graph & Symbol Engine
components.description: Builds a unified representation of the codebase by resolving LSP locations into a canonical symbol table, constructing the call graph, and resolving references back to source coordinates.

components.name: Multi-Language Adapter Framework
components.description: Normalization layer abstracting language-specific differences and providing adapters for name formatting, package hierarchies, and LSP initialization options per language.

components.name: Incremental State & Result Management
components.description: Persists and caches analysis outputs (symbol tables, call graphs, class hierarchies) and manages incremental updates to re-analyze only changed files.

components.name: Agentic Data Modeling & Visualization
components.description: Transforms persisted semantic graph data into AI-optimized models and visualizations (e.g., Pydantic insights, Cytoscape/Mermaid) for consumption by LLM agents and humans.

components.name: Multi-Agent Intelligence System
components.description: Reasoning layer of specialized AI agents (Meta, Abstraction, Details) which use a prompt factory and toolkit to query code and static analysis outputs and to generate architectural descriptions and diagrams.

components.name: Reasoning Orchestration
components.description: Manages the lifecycle and coordination of specialized AI agents. Defines the high-level reasoning strategy from metadata discovery to abstract component mapping and detailed architectural description, and maintains execution context and tracing for multi-step agentic workflows.

components.name: Agent Tooling & Persistence
components.description: Provides the tools and persistence layer used by agents. Includes the CodeBoardingToolkit for filesystem and graph querying, base agent execution logic with timeout handling and response parsing, and state persistence via DuckDB and local caching.

components.name: Prompt Factory & LLM Gateway
components.description: Centralized subsystem for prompt engineering and LLM provider abstraction. Serves optimized templates for architectural tasks and handles communication with multiple providers to ensure structured and consistent agent responses.

components.name: Architectural Mapping & CFG Bridge
components.description: Translation layer between deterministic static analysis and probabilistic AI reasoning. Provides algorithms for grouping CFG nodes into logical clusters and prepares Analysis Insights that summarize complex graph relationships for LLM consumption.

components.name: Repository Intelligence Layer
components.description: The "ground truth" data layer that provides raw architectural data. It aggregates language-specific adapters and repository scanning logic, handling low-level concerns like LSP diagnostics, git diffs, and source inspection to feed the agent toolkit.

components.name: Incremental State & Persistence
components.description: Handles caching, incremental analysis, impact detection, and job persistence (e.g., via DuckDB) to avoid reprocessing unchanged parts and to support long-running or remote tasks.

components.name: Static Analysis Cache Manager
components.description: Handles serialization, deserialization, and invalidation of static analysis data (call graphs, class hierarchies, diagnostics) and merges deterministic LSP data into a cache for efficient incremental runs.

components.name: Change Impact Analyzer
components.description: Detects file-level changes from version control and maps them to high-level architectural components, deciding whether a component is dirty (needs LLM re-analysis) or patchable based on the change's structural significance.

components.name: Incremental Update Orchestrator
components.description: Coordinates incremental updates, selecting between path patching, targeted component re-expansion, or full re-analysis when architecture boundaries change, and executes the chosen update strategy.

components.name: Job & Task Persistence
components.description: Persists and manages lifecycle of analysis jobs and metadata (e.g., via DuckDB) to support long-running or remote tasks, tracking status, results, and errors for observability.

components.name: State Validation & Agent Tooling
components.description: Provides inspection and validation tools for AI agents to query persisted state (CFG, structure, dependencies) and validates integrity after incremental updates to avoid inconsistencies.

components.name: Code Health & Diagnostics
components.description: Analyzes static data to identify architectural smells, circular dependencies, and maintainability metrics; produces health reports and integrates diagnostics into final outputs.

components.name: Health Orchestrator & CLI
components.description: Acts as the primary entry point and high-level coordinator. Manages CLI execution, initializes configuration, and prepares the repository environment (cloning, path normalization) for analysis.

components.name: Diagnostic Execution Engine
components.description: Central processing hub that manages the lifecycle of a health check run. Collects language-specific checks, filters source files based on exclusion patterns, and aggregates individual findings into a unified report.

components.name: Architectural Analysis Engine
components.description: Implements deterministic logic for identifying architectural smells and calculating maintainability metrics using static analysis (coupling, cohesion, dependency structures).

components.name: Incremental State Manager
components.description: Ensures diagnostic consistency across sessions by managing path-patching logic; allows updating existing health manifests when files are moved or renamed without full re-analysis.

components.name: Agentic Git & Change Analyzer
components.description: Provides agent-specific tools to analyze code changes via Git. Inspects diffs to support change-based diagnostics, allowing focus on modified areas.

components.name: Execution Monitoring & Tracing
components.description: Provides observability for the diagnostic process, tracking execution steps and LLM tool usage via context managers and callbacks to provide telemetry on performance and resource consumption.
